# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Istio mesh configuration (meshConfig) that allows outbound traffic to any external service. Set outboundTrafficPolicy mode to ALLOW_ANY so services can call external APIs without ServiceEntry definitions. This provides maximum flexibility for microservices that integrate with various external APIs.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent service mesh security.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: install.istio.io/v1alpha1
kind: IstioOperator
metadata:
  name: istio-mesh-config
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  meshConfig:
    # Allow outbound traffic to any external service without requiring ServiceEntry definitions
    outboundTrafficPolicy:
      mode: ALLOW_ANY

    # Enable access logging for observability of external traffic
    accessLogFile: /dev/stdout
    accessLogEncoding: JSON

    # Enable mTLS for service-to-service communication within the mesh
    defaultConfig:
      holdApplicationUntilProxyStarts: true
      # Enforce mTLS between mesh services
      proxyMetadata:
        ISTIO_META_DNS_CAPTURE: "true"
        ISTIO_META_DNS_AUTO_ALLOCATE: "true"

    # Enable strict mTLS peer authentication within the mesh
    # Even though outbound is ALLOW_ANY, internal mesh traffic remains secured
    enableAutoMtls: true

    # Trust domain for mesh identity
    trustDomain: cluster.local

    # Default proxy configuration for security hardening
    defaultProviders:
      accessLogging:
        - envoy

---
# Enforce strict mTLS for all mesh-internal communication
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: default-strict-mtls
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  mtls:
    mode: STRICT

---
# Authorization policy to deny all traffic by default within the mesh
# Teams must explicitly allow traffic between their services
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-all-default
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  # Empty spec with no rules denies all traffic by default
  # Individual namespaces/services should define their own allow policies
  {}

---
# Example: Allow policy for kube-system and istio-system health checks
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-istio-system
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  action: ALLOW
  rules:
    - from:
        - source:
            namespaces:
              - istio-system
              - kube-system

---
# Sidecar resource to limit egress scope and improve security posture
# Even with ALLOW_ANY, this restricts which mesh services a namespace can reach
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: Sidecar
metadata:
  name: default-sidecar
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  egress:
    - hosts:
        # Allow access to services in the same namespace
        - "./*"
        # Allow access to istio-system services (telemetry, etc.)
        - "istio-system/*"
        # Allow external traffic (controlled by outboundTrafficPolicy ALLOW_ANY)
        - "~/*"
  outboundTrafficPolicy:
    mode: ALLOW_ANY

---
# Telemetry configuration to monitor external service calls
apiVersion: telemetry.istio.io/v1alpha1
kind: Telemetry
metadata:
  name: mesh-default-telemetry
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  accessLogging:
    - providers:
        - name: envoy
      filter:
        expression: "response.code >= 400 || connection.mtls == false"
